reason: Both answers correctly recommend talking the team out of full event sourcing in favor of an append-only ledger + CRUD + idempotency keys, and both mention buying a billing provider as an alternative — covering all the key points accurately. Answer A is slightly more concise and gets to the point without a redundant preamble, and its reasoning about why full ES specifically hurts billing (re-rating historical invoices, eventual-consistency UX problems) is concrete. Answer B includes a "Sketch" section that duplicates content stated moments later, which is minor padding, but it adds a useful nuance about volume thresholds for when to consider buying vs. building. These differences roughly cancel out; both are correct, well-structured, and land on the same well-justified verdict.
